skill.name: diagnosing-bugs
skill.description: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Use when the user says "diagnose"/"debug this", or reports something broken/throwing/failing/slow.
skill.summary: Structured 6-phase debugging methodology for hard bugs and performance regressions.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: All 13 static analysis findings were evaluated as FALSE POSITIVES. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections (9 instances) are Markdown inline-code backticks in SKILL.md - not actual shell execution. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections (2 instances) matched on YAML front matter and a checklist item - no cryptographic code exists. The 'Hardcoded URL' points to http://localhost:3000 in a user-editable template script. The 'System reconnaissance' detection is a human-in-the-loop debugging question. The skill is a debugging methodology document and a bash template for structured user interaction. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or obfuscation detected.

content.user_title: Diagnose and Fix Tough Software Bugs
content.value_statement: Debugging complex bugs often leads to wasted time with unstructured trial and error. This skill provides a systematic phased methodology to build feedback loops, reproduce issues, and fix problems efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Guides the AI through a structured 6-phase debugging process from reproduction to fix
Builds tight feedback loops that reliably reproduce bugs with deterministic signals
Creates minimal reproduction cases by systematically removing non-load-bearing elements
Generates and ranks 3 to 5 falsifiable hypotheses before testing any single theory
Establishes performance baselines and uses targeted instrumentation for regression analysis
Creates regression tests and documents root cause findings for future reference
limitations: Cannot build a feedback loop when the bug environment is inaccessible or cannot be replicated
Cannot fix bugs that require changes in parts of the system outside the AI modification scope
Does not replace domain knowledge about the specific codebase framework or business logic
May not help with bugs that have extremely low reproduction rates and no available instrumentation

use_cases.title: Fix a Production Regression
use_cases.description: A web application started failing after a recent deployment. The skill guides the AI to build a reproduction loop, bisect the change, and create a fix with a regression test.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developer debugging a production issue

use_cases.title: Optimize Slow Database Queries
use_cases.description: A feature became unusable after a schema change. The skill helps establish a performance baseline, measure query plans, and identify the bottleneck through targeted instrumentation.

use_cases.title: Debug an Intermittent UI Bug
use_cases.description: A UI component sometimes renders with incorrect state. The skill helps build a loop with higher reproduction rate, narrow timing windows, and inject conditions to make the flaky bug debuggable.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developer dealing with non-deterministic bugs

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Simple Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: I am seeing an error when I submit the form on my website. The form does not save data to the database. Can you help me diagnose and fix this issue?
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer encounters a straightforward bug and needs guided debugging help

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Performance Regression
prompt_templates.prompt: The search feature on my app used to return results in under 200ms but now takes over 5 seconds. The change was deployed yesterday. Can you help me identify what caused the regression?
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer notices a performance degradation and needs systematic analysis

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a Complex Multi-Step Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Users report that their session expires randomly during checkout. The error only appears in production and I cannot reproduce it locally. Please help me build a feedback loop and diagnose the root cause.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer faces a hard-to-reproduce bug that spans multiple system components

prompt_templates.title: Full Debugging Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Our CI pipeline has been failing intermittently for the past week. The failure is not consistent across runs. I need a systematic approach to build a reliable reproduction, identify the root cause, and implement a fix with proper regression tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: A senior developer needs comprehensive debugging methodology for a systemic issue

output_examples.input: The login button does nothing when clicked
output: Phase 1 completed: Built a feedback loop by adding console.log to the click handler. Confirmed the click event fires but the API call is never made.
Phase 2: Reproduced the bug consistently. Minimized to a missing import in the authentication service.
Phase 3 to 5: Generated 3 hypotheses. Leading hypothesis confirmed as a circular dependency. Fix applied and regression test added.

output_examples.input: Search results take too long to load after yesterdays deploy
output: Baseline measurement established: search took 8.4 seconds with a cold cache.
Bisected the regression to a new database index. The index causes slow write operations that lock the search table.
Fix applied by optimizing the index query. Search now returns in 300ms. Performance regression test added.

best_practices: Build the feedback loop first before forming any hypotheses about the root cause of the bug
Minimize the reproduction case to only load-bearing elements before moving to hypothesis generation
Tag all debug instrumentation with unique prefixes so cleanup is systematic and thorough
anti_patterns: Jumping straight to a hypothesis without first building a working reproduction feedback loop
Changing multiple variables at once during the instrumentation phase of investigation
Adding untagged debug logs that accumulate in the codebase and are never cleaned up

faq.question: What is the most important step in debugging?
faq.answer: Building a tight feedback loop that can reliably reproduce the bug is the most critical step.

faq.question: How do I debug a bug that only happens in production?
faq.answer: Try to capture a trace, HAR file, or log dump from production. If that is not possible, ask for temporary production instrumentation.

faq.question: What should I do if I cannot reproduce the bug?
faq.answer: Keep working on the feedback loop. Try different approaches like increasing the reproduction rate for intermittent bugs or building a minimal harness.

faq.question: How many hypotheses should I generate before testing?
faq.answer: Generate 3 to 5 ranked hypotheses before testing any of them. This prevents anchoring on the first plausible idea.

faq.question: What is the difference between a feedback loop and a reproduction case?
faq.answer: A feedback loop is the mechanism that detects the bug. A reproduction case is the minimal input that makes the loop show the failure.

faq.question: How should I handle non-deterministic bugs?
faq.answer: Aim for a higher reproduction rate, not a perfect reproduction. Loop the trigger 100 times, parallelize, and narrow timing windows.
